transcript: The inspiration of the look of the show, Maria Grazia showed me a picture of a 60s woman in profile with big lashes and a big eyeliner and she said I like the space in between both liners. She wanted something which is not too vintage but still an eyeliner. For me, an eyeliner, whatever shape or form, it's always very Dior, it's very Paris. I need to find a solution for a makeup that matches 90 different types of girls. So we went for half an eye, but double it up, so we get a double eyeliner. We didn't add any colors or anything to make it look cute. That's why it's pure skin, just black and white on the eye. I used On Stage Liner in Black Vinyl, which is almost like a marker. And the space in between we filled up with the Beige Coal Liner. We start with the skin and then the foundation. Our backstage foundation, which is very manageable for all types of skin types. Where needed, a bit more concealer if you need a bit more extra coverage. The Skin Correct that we use under the eyes maybe. And then we gently enhance the eyebrows with the new Kabuki Brow Styler. You can go really sharp and you can blur out. And then you can really create a very natural eyebrow. The idea is to elongate the eyebrow in a way that it's harmonious with the eyeliner. On lips we just use the Rouge Dior lip balm, no tint, no color. And then just before they go up, I like to finish the skin with the Powder No Powder. It's not mattifying, but it creates like some sort of a film. It just sets the foundation and makes sure it's a beautiful luminous finish. And that's it.

timestamps.text: The inspiration of the look of the show, Maria Grazia showed me a picture of a 60s woman in profile with big lashes and a big eyeliner and she said I like the space in between both liners. She wanted something which is not too vintage but still an eyeliner. For me, an eyeliner, whatever shape or form, it's always very Dior, it's very Paris. I need to find a solution for a makeup that matches 90 different types of girls. So we went for half an eye, but double it up, so we get a double eyeliner. We didn't add any colors or anything to make it look cute. That's why it's pure skin, just black and white on the eye. I used On Stage Liner in Black Vinyl, which is almost like a marker. And the space in between we filled up with the Beige Coal Liner. We start with the skin and then the foundation. Our backstage foundation, which is very manageable for all types of skin types. Where needed, a bit more concealer if you need a bit more extra coverage. The Skin Correct that we use under the eyes maybe. And then we gently enhance the eyebrows with the new Kabuki Brow Styler. You can go really sharp and you can blur out. And then you can really create a very natural eyebrow. The idea is to elongate the eyebrow in a way that it's harmonious with the eyeliner.
On lips we just use the Rouge Dior lip balm, no tint, no color.
And then just before they go up, I like to finish the skin with the Powder No Powder.
It's not mattifying, but it creates like some sort of a film.
It just sets the foundation and makes sure it's a beautiful luminous finish.
